title: Kurdish Voices
subtitle: Dialect-specific automatic speech recognition for Kurdish

sections.heading: Overview
sections.value: Kurdish Voices is a project focused on building dialect-aware automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems for Kurdish. Existing multilingual ASR models - even those that nominally support Kurdish - are typically trained on a narrow slice of one dialect, meaning speakers of other varieties receive significantly degraded recognition quality. This project investigates targeted fine-tuning strategies that allow a base model to specialise for a specific Kurdish dialect while retaining robustness.

sections.heading: Motivation
sections.value: Spoken language is the primary mode of communication for many Kurdish speakers, particularly in rural and diaspora communities where literacy in Kurdish may be limited. High-quality dialect-specific ASR is therefore not merely an academic goal - it is a prerequisite for applications like voice interfaces, automatic transcription of oral history recordings, and accessibility tools. The current gap in dialect-aware Kurdish ASR is significant and largely unaddressed in the research literature.

sections.heading: Approach
items: Fine-tuning pre-trained multilingual speech models (e.g. Whisper-family) on dialect-specific Kurdish audio data.
Leveraging the dialect-specific text corpora produced by the Dialectal Filtering project as a language modelling resource.
Evaluating recognition quality per dialect using appropriate word error rate (WER) benchmarks.
[TODO: describe data collection strategy once finalised]

sections.heading: Current Status
sections.value: This project is in early development. Data collection and model selection are ongoing. Results and publications will be added here as the work progresses. [TODO: update with progress]

sections.heading: Connection to the Broader PhD Project
sections.value: Kurdish Voices is the spoken-language thread of the Dialect-Aware NLP PhD project, complementing the text-focused Dialectal Filtering work. Together they aim to cover both the written and spoken dimensions of Kurdish dialectal NLP.
